Specific data points used: 15% monthly revenue exposed to declines; >69% recovered => net leakage approx 4.5%. Netflix global ARM 2024 $11.70; Disney+ overall ARPU Q4 2024 $7.20; WBD DTC global ARPU Q4 2024 $7.44 for reasonableness.
Improvement: Converts list-price MRR into effective collected MRR and anchors ARPU to audited/operator-reported ranges.

Specific data points used: ARR = Effective MRR x 12 after 4.5% billing leakage adjustment.
Improvement: ARR now rolls up from a benchmark-calibrated MRR base.

Previous Source: Proxy using all-annual logic / simplified contract assumptions
New Source: Derived from effective ARPU and contract mix with benchmarked annualization
Specific data points used: ACV per acquired account = Effective ARPU x 12 x seat factor. Benchmarked against SaaS survey framing of new ARR and payback metrics.
Improvement: More consistent with annualized contract value economics and benchmark terminology.

New Source: Derived from ACV x weighted contract term
Specific data points used: Weighted contract term parameter retained as editable assumption; TCV = ACV x avg term years.
Improvement: Separates contract duration from annual value and preserves auditability.

New Source: Derived from net new subscriptions x ACV
Specific data points used: Net new subscriptions retained from prior model base; bookings tied directly to benchmark-calibrated ACV.
Improvement: Bookable value now linked to calibrated contract economics.

Previous Source: Hazard curve from Kaggle churn transfer
New Source: Calibrated renewal rate using industry churn benchmarks
Specific data points used: Streaming churn reference 2%–7%; SaaS/B2B benchmark ~3.5%; base blended churn 4.5% => retention 95.5%.
Improvement: Pulls renewals into industry-realistic ranges instead of raw Kaggle churn rates >44%.

Specific data points used: Cumulative resubscription curve: 10% next month, 23% within 3 months, 37% within 9 months, 41% within 12 months.
Improvement: Replaces fabricated win-back assumptions with an observed time-decay curve.

New Source: Benchmark-bounded range from KeyBanc and OpenView, plus ratio validation against 3.6x CLV/CAC median
Specific data points used: Blended CAC ratio ~$1.20 per $1 new ARR; new customer CAC ratio ~$2.00; CLV/CAC median 3.6.
Improvement: Moves CAC from arbitrary point estimate to benchmark-bounded and ratio-validated metric.

New Source: Gross-margin-adjusted revenue lifetime validated against 3.6x benchmark
Specific data points used: Gross margin benchmark 79%; CLV/CAC validation target 3.6.
Improvement: CLV formula remains transparent and is now tested against an external benchmark.

New Source: Retained Kaggle/legacy model base, benchmark checked against benchmark ARPU and downstream economics
Specific data points used: Base monthly net new subscriptions set to 40 from original forecast centerline; editable input.
Improvement: Preserves the existing acquisition engine while improving downstream monetization realism.

New Source: Gross churn less Antenna-style reactivation credit and expansion offsets
Specific data points used: Base gross churn 4.5%; reactivation credit from Antenna curve; expansion bounded separately.
Improvement: Net churn is now decomposed into gross churn, reactivation, and expansion rather than treated as a black box.

Specific data points used: Expansion ARR = 40% of total new ARR overall; >50% for >$50M ARR; ChartMogul expansion share rising toward low-30s of ARR gained.
Improvement: Replaces fabricated expansion with benchmark-bounded expansion economics.

Previous Source: Kaggle churn labels with unrealistic 44%–52% churn
Specific data points used: Use calibrated monthly churn band 2%–7%; base blended churn 4.5% => retention 95.5%.
Improvement: Fixes the largest domain-transfer issue in the original model.

New Source: Retained proxy (editable) because no better free public row-level seat dataset was identified
Primary URL: Retained from original Kaggle-style feature design
Specific data points used: Seat factor default = 1.00, fully editable to reflect customer mix.
Improvement: Maintains transparency: seat economics remain proxy-based and user-editable rather than disguised as observed data.
